ending6.title: Ending 06 - Bystander
dialogue: A massive scythe scrapes the floor, trailing sparks before coming to a dead halt.
The form of the Reaper, the absolute ruler of this world, scatters into a black mist, leaving his grotesque throne to cast a hollow shadow.
The Necromancer, who once reigned over an entire world, slowly lowers his blood-stained hand, staring blankly through the dissipating smoke.
Revenge is complete.
Though he has severed the breath of the god who insulted him and dragged him here, neither joy nor a sense of accomplishment dwells in his eyes.
Only a piercing, frozen indifference lingers there.
He turns his gaze downward to the terminal, now a scene of utter carnage.
With the Reaper's death, the gargantuan gears that maintained the order of this world grind to a halt with a deafening shriek.
Trains derail and collide with massive steel structures, and exploding flames dye the dark sky a bruised crimson.
Amidst the chaotic landscape where countless souls and supplies are crushed and mangled, he does not move a single inch.
He stands at the edge of a crumbling railing, merely watching the scenery of ruin.
He makes no move to save anyone, nor does he take pleasure in the destruction.
To him, from beginning to end, this place was nothing more than a labor camp filled with filth that needed to be cleared.
Now, the Reaper has simply been added to the list of things to be swept away.
In the place where the chains were loosed, a suffocating silence arrives instead of freedom.
The world he came from has long since perished or forgotten him,
And in this collapsing realm, no one remains to celebrate his victory.
The protagonist slowly sinks to the floor.
Behind him, the walls of the office crumble, kicking up massive clouds of dust, but he does not turn his head.
The light from the burning terminal stretches his shadow long before swallowing it into the deepening darkness.
In the heart of a wasteland where no one remains, he watches the world's collapse to the very end, fossilized within the vast void he created.

ending5.title: Ending 05 - New Order
dialogue: The chaos visible through the office window slowly subsides.
The flames of the revolutionary army, which once threatened to consume the entire depot, have fallen asleep amidst the cold steel and rubble.
In front of the Reaper's throne, only the dark stains on the floor prove that the master of this place has changed.
The protagonist tidies his bloodied sleeves and picks up a massive ring of keys—the Reaper's legacy.
The heavy office doors swing open, and individuals dressed in refined formal wear step inside cautiously.
These are the VIPs who were rescued unharmed by the protagonist's intervention amidst the revolutionary fires.
They do not spare a glance for the remains of the dead scattered at their feet or the screams echoing from the ruined terminal.
They are concerned only with their own safety and the resumption of the halted train services.
With elegant gestures, they pay their respects to the protagonist and urge him to fill the vacancy left by the Reaper.
To the protagonist, who was repulsed by the radicals of the resistance who justified innocent sacrifices for the 'greater good',
Their proposal offers a strange sense of relief, yet simultaneously, a cold sense of restraint.
The protagonist looks down at the endless tracks stretching beyond the window.
The mindless souls begin to board the trains in orderly lines once more, and the halted machinery starts to throb with a rhythmic pulse.
The 'liberation' the revolutionaries dreamed of is gone, but in its place, a rigid and sophisticated 'order' takes root again.
He slowly leans back against the Reaper's throne.
As long as this terminal exists, and until someone more powerful drags him from this seat,
He is merely a new administrator, subordinate to this gargantuan iron cradle.
From the protagonist's hand, a massive horn blast signals the departure of the trains.
All the lights of the terminal flicker on at once, and as the world returns to its busy cycle, he gazes out with the heavy eyes of one wearing a crown.

ending4.title: Ending 04 - Tyranny
dialogue: The marble floors of the terminal are so deeply stained with crimson that their original color is unrecognizable.
Having crushed the Reaper's throat and usurped his power, the protagonist sits upon the ruined throne, his blood-soaked cloak fluttering.
The massive doors of the office are hideously twisted. Beyond them, the form of the absolute being who ruled for eons scatters like unidentifiable ash.
The Necromancer stares into the void with hollow eyes, as if he has already forgotten that he just murdered a god.
To him, this massacre and conquest were not great achievements, but merely the clearing of bothersome dust that blocked his path.
Outside the terminal windows, terrified citizens and surviving spirits flee in a panicked frenzy.
The necromantic mana surging from his fingertips sweeps through the entire depot, instantly draining the life from those attempting to escape.
Now, this terminal is no longer a hub connecting worlds, but a massive slaughterhouse breeding and butchering the living for a single tyrant.
To the background noise of the terminal's engine throbbing like a mechanical heartbeat, the protagonist commands his resurrected legion of the dead to invade the next world.
The man who did not fear even the Reaper has now become Death itself, declaring an eternal reign of darkness while trampling all the order of this world under his feet.

ending3.title: Ending 03 - Hound of Death
dialogue: The disturbances at the terminal have been perfectly suppressed.
The banners of the resistance, which tried to derail the system, have been torn apart and turned into rags to wipe the filth from the floor.
The Necromancer stands at attention in the center of the office, having executed the Reaper's orders with absolute loyalty and utter cruelty.
Now, in this terminal, only the vibrations of trains departing at their scheduled times and mechanical control remain.
The Reaper wears an indiscernible, satisfied smile upon his throne.
The Necromancer receives that gaze directly, without even a blink of an eye.
The pride of a ruler who once commanded a world, and the burning vengeance once directed at a god, are nowhere to be found.
His eyes are sunken, transparent and empty, much like the souls that wander the terminal without a sense of self.
He kneels at the Reaper's feet, accepting a new command issued by Death.
What is granted to him is not freedom, but an intensification of eternal labor.
He has fossilized himself into the sharpest blade, a hound of death that eliminates all variables threatening this massive hub.
The terminal's display boards flicker with regular numbers once more, and he heads toward the dark platforms, clutching a broom in one hand and a blade in the other.
There is no hesitation, no emotion in his footsteps.
Like a sophisticated doll moving only by its master's will, he fades back into the darkness to restore the broken order.

ending2.title: Ending 02 - Collapse
dialogue: At the heart of the terminal, the massive power room connecting dimensions twists with a grotesque shriek.
At his feet, the torn shroud of the Reaper and the shattered fragments of his soul scatter like dust.
He has executed the one who dragged him into this pit of filth, but what dwells in his eyes is not a victor's joy, but a profound, weary boredom.
Nothing in this world interests him anymore.
The indiscriminate violence he unleashed has crushed everyone—both the administrators who guarded the order and the rebels who dreamed of change.
The master is dead, and the gargantuan machine he managed loses control, beginning to spiral into a violent overload.
The massive tracks crossing the sky snap under the weight, crashing down upon the platforms.
Lost souls scatter into the air with screams, and the terminal's portals, which once linked worlds, emit unstable mana, swallowing everything in their vicinity.
He looks up at the collapsing ceiling and lightly brushes off his hands.
Even as the ground beneath his feet splits open to reveal the boiling core of this world, he makes no attempt to avoid it.
To him, this world was merely an unpleasant prison that confined him, and the sight of its collapse is nothing more than the final task of a day's work.
Every light in the terminal flickers out simultaneously, bringing a profound darkness.
The flow of mana that formed the foundation of the world is severed, and space itself begins to crumple like paper.
In the center of the vortex of a perishing dimension, he watches the scene grow cold, as if he had never existed from the start.

ending1.title: Ending 01 - Revolution
dialogue: The heavy black clouds that weighed down the terminal sky disperse, and a transparent light, seen for the first time, illuminates every corner of the platforms.
The Reaper's throne, which for eons squeezed the lifeblood out of the dead, has crumbled into dust, and in its place echo the cheers of those who were once oppressed.
The Necromancer receives the looks of gratitude and awe, tidying his cloak stained with blood and dust.
Mindless souls no longer wander the tracks that were once filled with filth.
The victory achieved hand-in-hand with the revolutionary army has restored all systems of this world to 'rest' and 'cycle' instead of 'labor'.
The small mercy shown by the protagonist became a spark that grew into a massive blaze, melting all the rusted chains that bound the terminal.
The leaders of the revolutionary army approach the protagonist, declaring victory and appointing him as the 'Great Administrator'.
The halted trains now begin to carry the dead not to scenes of suffering, but to the peaceful return they truly deserve.
The dawn light filtering through the broken walls seems to prove that this place is no longer a prison, but a 'terminal' in the truest sense of a new beginning.
He remains alone in the stillness of the depot, looking down at his own hands.
The chains are broken, but the scars etched into his skin remain cold.
In the long night that follows after the festival's fireworks have died down, he silently wonders: Is this position they've granted him a door to be opened, or a lock to confine him here forever?
The Reaper's massive frame finally collapses, shaking the floor of the office.
An era of tyranny comes to an end, and the immense spiritual pressure that stifled the terminal is released at once.
The revolutionary army embraces one another in the joy of victory, and the dead finally begin to head toward their rest along the destined tracks.
The Necromancer, stained with blood and dust, stands alone at the end of the platform, staring at the endless rails.
He recalls a story he chanced upon during his time of labor.
It was not about reincarnation into the afterlife or eternal life in this realm, but a path to 'extinction'—erasing all traces of existence to reach eternal peace.
Now that the order of the terminal has collapsed with the Reaper's death, that forbidden door opens only for him.
The leaders approach him, reaching out their hands and asking him to be the foundation of the newly established world.
In their eyes lies both hope for a new era and a longing for the absolute power that will sustain it.
However, the protagonist looks beyond their reaching hands, toward a distant rift of void where all color and sound vanish.

The arrogance of a ruler and the humiliation of a slave are now meaningless. Leaving behind the shining scenery of the revolution he achieved, he stands before the threshold of eternal silence, just a single step away.
Will he remain here to become the god of a new history, or will he sever all karmic ties and return to absolute peace?
The train's horn echoes from afar, and only the right to choose his fate grows cold within his palm.
ending0.confirm: Will you leave this place? (The save file will be deleted)
leave: Leaving behind the hands extended by the leaders, his footsteps head not toward the shining platforms, but to the end of the tracks where darkness gathers.
The bewildered shouts and cheers from behind grow distant, and a space of silence, untouched even by the noise of this realm, unfolds before his eyes.
Beyond the silence lies a pure abyss, neither paradise nor hell, where nothing is defined.
The memories of ruling, the pain of labor, and even the futility that remained after revenge... all karma begins to scatter like smoke.
He feels his silhouette becoming transparent and blurred, letting out a peaceful breath that feels weightless for the first time in his life.
The terminal's tracks can no longer bind him. He throws himself into the vortex of eternal rest, placing the final period on his own story.
When every light in this world flickers out and only a profoundly lonely silence remains, not even a handful of dust is left where he once stood.
stay: Declining the ornate robes and the administrator's keys offered by the leaders, he quietly shakes his head.
Leaving behind their persistent requests to ascend the throne and establish a new order, he slips away from the noisy festival to settle in a quiet corner of the platform.
Neither the flames of revenge nor the desire to rule remain; they have turned to ash without a trace.
Feeling the wind of this world blowing through the broken outer walls, he watches the trajectories of the busy trains for the first time without any purpose.
Watching the dead, who have regained their sense of self and are heading toward their respective paths, a very faint and unfamiliar smile spreads across his lips.
He could have leapt into the door of Nirvana right then, but he decided to enjoy this strange peace a little longer.
As he gently strokes his wrists where the chains once were, his heart is as calm and still as the surface of water after a storm.
Without rushing, he sits and begins to silently watch the process of this peculiar terminal—the place that once bound him—being dyed in the hues of true rest.
And after that, no one ever saw him again.
ending0.title: Ending 00 - Nirvana
